title: Chapter 9 Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of forms and input widgets!

questions.question: Which widget is used for single-line text input?
questions.explanation: TextField and TextFormField both handle text input; TextFormField integrates with Form.

questions.question: InputDecoration is used to style a TextField.
questions.explanation: Yes, it provides label, hint, border, etc.

questions.question: To group and validate multiple fields, use the _____ widget.
questions.explanation: Form manages multiple form fields.

questions.question: How do you access the FormState?
questions.explanation: You need a GlobalKey assigned to the Form's key parameter.

questions.question: A validator function should return a string if the input is invalid.
questions.explanation: Return null for valid, a non-null error message for invalid.

questions.question: When is the validator called?
options: When validate() is called on the FormState
questions.explanation: validate() triggers all validators.

questions.question: The onSaved callback is called when you call _____ on the FormState.
questions.explanation: save() triggers onSaved for all fields.

questions.question: A Checkbox allows multiple selections in a group.
questions.explanation: Checkboxes are independent; each can be toggled on/off.

questions.question: Which widget is used for mutually exclusive options?
questions.explanation: Radio buttons are for mutually exclusive choices.

questions.question: To make a TextField obscure text (like a password), set _____ to true.
questions.explanation: obscureText: true hides the input.

questions.question: Slider works with integer values only.
questions.explanation: Slider uses double values; you can use divisions for discrete int steps.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'items' property in DropdownButton?
options: To define the list of selectable options
questions.explanation: items takes a list of DropdownMenuItem widgets.

questions.question: To update the UI when a form field changes, you typically call _____.
questions.explanation: setState triggers a rebuild with the new value.

questions.question: A TextEditingController must be disposed to avoid memory leaks.
questions.explanation: Dispose it in the State's dispose method.

questions.question: Which property of TextField controls the keyboard type (e.g., email, number)?
questions.explanation: keyboardType sets the type of keyboard to show.

questions.question: To show a dropdown that integrates with Form, use _____.
questions.explanation: DropdownButtonFormField is the Form-aware version.

questions.question: The 'onChanged' callback of a form field is called when the value changes.
questions.explanation: onChanged fires on every change, useful for real-time updates.

questions.question: How do you reset all fields in a form?
questions.explanation: reset() restores fields to their initial values.

questions.question: To validate a form automatically as the user interacts, set _____.
questions.explanation: This triggers validation on user interaction.

questions.question: A Switch widget has a boolean value.
questions.explanation: Switch's value is a bool, toggled via onChanged.

questions.question: What does the 'decoration' property of InputDecoration do?
options: Adds an icon, label, hint, and border
questions.explanation: InputDecoration handles visual aspects of the input field.

questions.question: To listen to text changes without a controller, use the _____ callback.
questions.explanation: onChanged gives you the current text value.

questions.question: A Radio button requires a groupValue to know which option is selected.
questions.explanation: All radios in a group share the same groupValue variable.

questions.question: Which widget allows selecting a value from a continuous range?
questions.explanation: Slider for single value, RangeSlider for a range.

questions.question: To make a TextField expand to multiple lines, set _____ to >1.
questions.explanation: maxLines specifies the maximum number of lines; null for unlimited.
